simulator.hero_title: Which cyber regulations apply to you?
simulator.hero_text: Answer a few questions and immediately discover which frameworks are applicable or recommended for your organisation.
simulator.organization_subtitle: These 6 questions determine the applicable regulations
simulator.q1_label: Where is your registered office / main activity?*
simulator.q1_option_eu_clients: Outside EU but clients/users in the EU
simulator.q2_option_ict_b2b: ICT B2B services (MSP, MSSP, managed services)
simulator.q2_option_digital_providers: Digital providers (marketplaces, search engines, social networks)
simulator.q3_option_small: Small: fewer than 50 employees AND turnover <€10M AND balance sheet <€10M
simulator.q3_option_medium: Medium: 50-249 employees OR turnover €10-50M OR balance sheet €10-43M
simulator.q3_option_large: Large: 250+ employees OR turnover >€50M OR balance sheet >€43M
simulator.disclaimer: This simulator provides a general indication and does not constitute legal advice.
simulator.q2_label: What is your main business sector?*
simulator.q3_label: What is the size of your organisation?*
simulator.q3_hint: NIS 2: <50 employees AND <€10M = not in scope. Any single threshold exceeded suffices.
simulator.q4_label: Do you process personal data?*
simulator.q4_hint: Name, email, phone, IP, cookies, HR data, customer files... 95% of companies = Yes
simulator.q5_label: Do you hold a financial licence (AMF, ACPR, CSSF...)?*
simulator.q5_hint: DORA targets exclusively regulated financial entities. The 'banking/finance' sector alone is NOT sufficient without a licence!
simulator.q6_label: Do you provide IT/Cloud services to clients?*
simulator.q6_hint: MSP, managed services, SaaS, hosting, IT consulting...
simulator.conditional_subtitle: These questions refine your diagnosis based on your profile
simulator.q7_label: Are you an ICT service provider for regulated financial entities?
simulator.q7_hint: Critical IT subcontractor for banks, insurers, asset managers... → DORA applies
simulator.q8_nis2_label: Is your entity a DNS provider, TLD registry, trust service provider, or telecom operator?
simulator.q8_nis2_hint: These entities are subject to NIS 2 regardless of size (Art. 2§2)
simulator.q8_label: Do you host health data for third parties (clients)?
simulator.q8_hint: For clients (hospitals, doctors, clinics). NOT your own internal data.
simulator.q9_label: Do you store/process bank card numbers (PAN)?
simulator.q9_hint: NOT applicable if you use Stripe/PayPal without ever seeing the numbers.
simulator.q10_label: Do you have activities or clients in the United States?*
simulator.q10a_label: Do your US clients require certifications (ISO 27001 / SOC 2)?
simulator.q10b_label: Do you work with the US Department of Defense (DoD)?
simulator.q11_label: Do you have critical activities requiring a business continuity plan?*
simulator.continuity_note: NIS 2 / DORA requires business continuity measures (Art. 21). A plan will be required even if you don't have one yet.

why.rgpd: Processing personal data with activity in the EU or EU clients/users
why.dora_regulated: EU financial entity holding a licence (AMF, ACPR, CSSF or equivalent)
why.dora_tic: Critical ICT service provider supplying EU regulated financial entities
why.nis2_eu_annex1_ee: Annex I sector (highly critical) + Sufficient size + EU registered → Essential Entity
why.nis2_eu_annex1_ei: Annex I sector (highly critical) + Sufficient size + EU registered → Important Entity
why.nis2_eu_annex2_ee: Annex II sector (critical) + Sufficient size + EU registered → Essential Entity
why.nis2_eu_annex2_ei: Annex II sector (critical) + Sufficient size + EU registered → Important Entity
why.nis2_extraterritorial: NIS 2 extra-territorial effect (Art. 26): entity outside EU providing services to EU clients
why.nis2_essential_small: NIS 2 Art. 2§2 exception: essential entity subject regardless of size (DNS provider, TLD registry, trust service provider, telecom operator)
why.pcidss: Storage, processing or transmission of bank card numbers (PAN)
why.hds: Hosting health personal data on behalf of third parties
why.iso27001_mandatory: Certification contractually required by your clients
why.iso27001_recommended: International reference standard for information security
why.soc2_mandatory: Attestation required by your US clients
why.soc2_recommended: Standard expected by US clients for SaaS/Cloud providers
why.iso22301_dora_nis2: DORA and NIS 2 require business continuity — ISO 22301 is the reference framework to comply
why.iso22301_dora: DORA requires business continuity (Art. 11-12) — ISO 22301 is the reference framework to comply
why.iso22301_nis2: NIS 2 requires business continuity (Art. 21§2c) — ISO 22301 is the reference framework to comply
why.iso22301_critical_sector: Critical sector — business continuity plan strongly recommended
why.iso22301_critical_activity: Critical activities identified — business continuity plan recommended
why.cmmc: Direct contracts or subcontracting with the US Department of Defense (DoD)

simulator.scope_text:
This assessment covers 5 standards: GDPR, DORA, NIS 2, ISO 27001, ISO 22301.
Other regulations (PCI-DSS, HDS, SOC 2, CMMC…) may apply depending on your context but are not evaluated here.
simulator.no_results_text: Based on your answers, we have not identified any mandatory regulation.
simulator.nis2_extraterritorial_note: Sectors subject to NIS 2 extra-territorial effect: (1) Digital infrastructure (IXP, DNS, TLD, datacentres), (2) ICT B2B services (MSP, MSSP, managed services), (3) Digital providers (marketplaces, search engines, social networks).
simulator.pdf_text: Get a PDF summary with all regulations, risks and deadlines.
simulator.pdf_popup_blocked: Popup blocked. Please allow popups for this site.
simulator.cta_text: Get a personalised audit and a compliance roadmap.

rgpd.sanctions: Up to €20M or 4% of global annual turnover

nis2.sanctions: Essential Entities: up to €10M or 2% turnover | Important Entities: up to €7M or 1.4% turnover

pcidss.fullName: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
pcidss.sanctions: $5K-$100K/month depending on duration + card processing withdrawal

iso27001.sanctions: Loss of contracts, exclusion from tenders

iso22301.sanctions: Risk of extended outage, financial losses

cmmc.sanctions: Exclusion from DoD contracts + False Claims Act fines

simulator.pdf_audit_section_title: Would you like to implement one or more audits?
simulator.pdf_audit_section_text: To implement the standards identified in this diagnosis, I offer personalised audits tailored to your context. Contact me at the address below to discuss your needs.

grc.title: GRC Cybersecurity — AgileVizion
grc.hero_h1: 10 million euros in fines or 2 % of global revenue — and that's just NIS 2
grc.hero_subtitle: DORA (Digital Operational Resilience Act), NIS 2 (Network and Information Security Directive 2), ISO 27001, GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) — every month a new requirement, every breach a penalty. I guide you from audit to compliance.
grc.problems_title: Are you asking yourself these questions?
grc.problem_1_title: Which regulations apply to my company?
grc.problem_1_text: DORA, NIS 2, GDPR, ISO 27001, CIS Controls... The proliferation of frameworks makes it difficult to identify your actual obligations — and those that don't concern you.
grc.problem_2_text: Some regulations expose you to severe financial and criminal penalties. Others are simply best practices. Confusing the two is costly — either through over-investment or fines.
grc.problem_3_text: The fines set by EU regulations are massive:
grc.problem_3_nis2: up to €10 M or 2 % of global annual turnover
grc.problem_3_rgpd: up to €20 M or 4 % of global annual turnover
grc.problem_4_title: What is the risk for executives?
grc.problem_4_text: DORA and NIS 2 introduce personal liability for executives: individual fines, mandatory cyber-risk training obligations, and possible dismissal in case of serious breach.
grc.solution_title: The solution: structured audit and compliance
grc.solution_text: I transform regulatory complexity into a clear, prioritised and actionable plan — without mobilising your teams for months.
grc.solution_point1: Precise identification of your actual regulatory obligations
grc.solution_point2: Methodical field audit with evidence collection and professional deliverables
grc.solution_point3: Prioritised roadmap by criticality, effort and impact
grc.approach_subtitle: A structured methodology aligned with ISO 19011 (Guidelines for auditing management systems).
grc.approach_1_text: Documentary and on-site controls, stakeholder interviews, evidence collection. Structured grid of 100 to 160+ control points per standard.
grc.approach_2_text: Classification of each requirement: compliant, non-compliant or partially compliant. Precise identification of gaps and their criticality.
grc.approach_3_text: Action plan prioritised by criticality, effort and regulatory deadline. Each gap is documented with a concrete recommendation and a designated owner.
grc.approach_4_text: Compliance implementation oversight: progress tracking, arbitration, stakeholder coordination. Optional, subject to availability.
grc.scope_subtitle: One consultant for DORA, NIS 2, ISO 27001 and ISO 22301 — no coordination between 3 firms, no context loss between phases.
grc.scope_iso27001_ref: Information Security Management System · ISO 27001:2022 · ISO 27002 · ISO 27005 · ISO 19011
grc.scope_iso27001_impl_1: Scope, organisational context and interested parties (clauses 4.1 to 4.4)
grc.scope_iso27001_impl_2: Security policy, objectives and leadership (clauses 5 and 6)
grc.scope_iso27001_impl_4: SoA (Statement of Applicability) and selection of 93 Annex A controls (ISO 27002)
grc.scope_iso27001_impl_5: Operational documentation: risk register, procedures, competencies
grc.scope_iso27001_audit_1: Internal audit per ISO 19011: interviews, documentary review, non-conformity grading (minor / major / critical)
grc.scope_iso27001_audit_2: Performance evaluation and management review (clauses 9.1 to 9.3)
grc.scope_dora_ref: Digital Operational Resilience Act · EU Regulation 2022/2554 · RTS/ITS · CSSF Circulars
grc.scope_dora_1: Gap analysis across 5 pillars: ICT risk management, incidents, resilience testing, third-party risk, information sharing
grc.scope_dora_2: Maturity rating 1 to 4 per requirement — identification of critical gaps
grc.scope_dora_3: Prioritised remediation plan with implementation roadmap
grc.scope_dora_4: Complete document corpus: DORA Regulation, RTS/ITS (Regulatory/Implementing Technical Standards), CSSF Circulars
grc.scope_dora_5: Critical ICT provider framing: contractual clauses, due diligence, exit strategy
grc.scope_nis2_ref: Network and Information Security Directive 2 · EU Directive 2022/2555 · Art. 21 (10 measures)
grc.scope_nis2_1: Assessment of 10 Art. 21 measures: security policies, risk analysis, incident management, business continuity
grc.scope_nis2_2: Supply chain security and third-party management
grc.scope_22301_bia_1: Activity-by-activity analysis: identification of critical processes
grc.scope_22301_bcp_1: 9 mandatory elements: scope, roles/RACI, activation, degraded procedures, fallback premises, critical resources, call tree, alternate suppliers, return to normal
grc.scope_22301_bcp_2: Executable by any designated employee without expert or network access
grc.scope_22301_drp_1: Failover to backup site, backup restoration, return to primary site
grc.scope_22301_drp_2: RTO/RPO aligned with BIA — each step executable by IT staff without external support
grc.scope_22301_test_1_desc: Fictional scenario, decision chain testing and BCP blind spot identification
grc.scope_22301_test_3_desc: Gap report vs BIA objectives + remediation plan
grc.scope_iso27001_s1: ISMS implementation: scope, policy, risks, SoA, 93 Annex A controls
grc.scope_iso27001_s2: Internal audit per ISO 19011: non-conformity grading, management review
grc.scope_iso27001_s3: Remediation, certification support and three-year cycle
grc.scope_dora_s1: Gap analysis across 5 pillars: ICT risks, incidents, resilience, third parties, sharing
grc.scope_dora_s2: Maturity rating 1 to 4 + prioritised remediation plan
grc.scope_dora_s3: Critical ICT provider framing: contractual clauses, due diligence, exit strategy
grc.scope_nis2_s1: 10 Art. 21 measures: policies, risks, incidents, continuity, supply chain
grc.scope_nis2_s2: Incident notification 24h / 72h + MFA, encryption, access control
grc.scope_22301_s1: BIA (Business Impact Analysis): critical processes, MTPD, RTO, RPO
grc.scope_22301_s2: BCP (Business Continuity Plan) + DRP (IT Disaster Recovery Plan)
grc.scope_22301_s3: Testing: tabletop exercise, DRP test, actual RTO/RPO measurement
grc.deliverable_1_text: 100 to 160+ control points per standard, with status (compliant / non-compliant / partial) and supporting evidence.
grc.deliverable_2_text: Management summary with key indicators, technical detail for security teams, and signed closing certificate.
grc.deliverable_3_text: Actions ranked by criticality, estimated effort and regulatory deadline. Each action is traceable to a gap.
grc.deliverable_4_text: Ready-to-use templates (ISMS policy, incident procedures, DPIA) + clear presentations per standard for management and business teams.
grc.frameworks_subtitle: Complete audit kits, tested and ready to use
grc.cta_title: Ready to gain clarity on your compliance?

ia.title: Agentic AI — AgileVizion
ia.hero_h1: Automate your business processes with dedicated AI agents
ia.hero_subtitle: From on-demand assistance to fully autonomous execution — you choose the level of autonomy. Humans keep control, provide expert judgement and drive continuous improvement.
ia.problems_subtitle: The research is consistent: your teams' cognitive time is wasted on mechanical tasks.
ia.kpi_p1_label: of working time spent on repetitive, low-value tasks
ia.kpi_p2_label: of employees lack time to do the strategic work expected of them
ia.kpi_p3_label: more errors made under pressure and cognitive overload
ia.consequence_2: Costly errors and omissions in document workflows
ia.consequence_3: Talent under-utilised, frustrated by mechanical tasks
ia.consequence_4: Growth capacity limited without additional headcount
ia.how_title: An AI agent that adapts to your autonomy needs
ia.how_subtitle: Every business process is different. The AI agent adjusts: from on-demand assistance to fully autonomous execution — you set the dial.
ia.autonomy_1_desc: The agent prepares and suggests — the human decides and executes at every step.
ia.autonomy_2_desc: The agent executes the full process — the human validates at key checkpoints before delivery.
ia.autonomy_3_desc: The agent handles the entire process — the human intervenes only on exceptions or periodic reviews.
ia.control_message: Humans always stay in control. They bring the value: quality review, expert judgement, final decision. The agent amplifies — it does not replace.
ia.example_subtitle: An AI agent works in 2 coordinated phases to produce a complete, traceable compliance audit.
ia.phase_1_item_1: Searching and downloading regulatory texts (DORA, NIS 2, ISO 27001…)
ia.phase_1_item_3: Verification against 7 quality criteria: completeness, accuracy, traceability, relevance, currency, format, usability
ia.phase_1_item_4: Automatic structuring into a knowledge base
ia.phase_2_item_1: Automatic pre-filling of the audit grid (100 to 160+ control points)
ia.phase_2_item_2: Generation of executive and technical reports
ia.phase_2_item_4: The consultant reviews, adjusts and signs — expert judgement is preserved
ia.jobs_subtitle: Here's what agentic AI concretely delivers in our engagements.
ia.job_1_s1: End-to-end GRC audit assisted by AI agents
ia.job_1_s3: Automatic OWASP scan before each delivery
ia.job_2_s1: Full AI agent team: Architect → Dev → QA → Review → DevOps
ia.job_3_s3: Integration with your existing tools (ERP, CRM, API)
ia.job_1_item_1: End-to-end audit assistance across the entire process
ia.job_1_item_2: Production of complete reference deliverables (client and auditor)
ia.job_1_item_5: Human control and review across the entire chain
ia.job_1_item_6: Customised process tailored to each client
ia.job_1_item_7: Integrated cybersecurity audit — every delivery goes through an automatic OWASP scan before release
ia.job_2_item_3: Orchestrated multi-role workflow: Architect → BA → Dev → QA → Review → Cyber → DevOps — a complete AI team, turnkey
ia.job_2_item_4: Intelligent error routing: each bug is classified and redirected to the right specialist (dev, debug or reviewer)
ia.job_2_item_5: ~200 automated tests per project (QA + OWASP + MITRE ATT&CK)
ia.job_2_item_7: Self-improvement loop: the agent analyses its own errors and improves its practices with each project
ia.job_4_item_2: Integration with your existing tools (ERP, CRM, databases)
ia.job_4_item_5: Systematic human control — the agent amplifies, it doesn't replace
ia.quality_title: Far beyond a simple AI chat
ia.quality_subtitle: A prompt in ChatGPT can generate code. But who checks the quality, the security, the resilience? Our pipeline does it automatically.
ia.quality_before_5: If it breaks, it's your problem
ia.quality_after_2: Systematic unit, integration and e2e tests
ia.quality_after_3: OWASP + MITRE ATT&CK audit on every delivery
ia.quality_after_5: Iterative loop: nothing ships without passing tests
ia.quality_after_6: Continuous self-improvement — the agent learns from its mistakes
ia.stack_subtitle: Our agents leverage the best AI models and automation tools on the market.
ia.tech_1_desc: State-of-the-art language models — advanced reasoning, complex document analysis, structured content production.
ia.tech_2_desc: AI agent dedicated to code — develops, tests, debugs and deploys with human review at every step.
ia.tech_3_desc: Specialised agents collaborating on complex tasks — each with its role, one shared objective.
ia.tech_4_desc: Open standard connecting agents to your existing tools — databases, APIs, files, browsers.
ia.tech_5_desc: Automated navigation, data collection, end-to-end testing on websites and web applications.
ia.tech_6_desc: Scheduling, state management, quality checks, business pipeline integration.
ia.approach_subtitle: A structured, agile and iterative process — from strategic framing to continuous improvement.
ia.approach_1_text: Strategic framing: sponsors, measurable objectives, scope. We set the rules before we start.
ia.approach_2_text: 1 role, 1 concrete business process. We pick the highest-ROI (Return on Investment) use case to demonstrate value fast.
ia.approach_3_text: Breakdown into tasks with inputs, outputs and progression states. We identify what's automatable vs what stays human.
ia.approach_4_text: Short iterations — we deliver a first result within days, then refine. Each iteration produces a usable deliverable.
ia.approach_5_text: User feedback, performance metrics, iterative adjustments. The agent improves with each cycle — then we move to the next process.
ia.process_desc: Each process is broken down into traceable tasks. At any point, you know the progression status, which inputs are required and which outputs are delivered.
ia.results_subtitle: Observed on agentic AI projects deployed in real-world contexts
ia.result_kpi_2_label: of time spent on document production
ia.result_kpi_3_label: omissions — quality control is systematic and traceable
ia.cta_title: Ready to identify what AI can do for you?
ia.cta_subtitle: 30-minute diagnostic — free, no commitment. We identify together the 2-3 most automatable tasks in your context.

blog.hero_subtitle: DORA, NIS 2, ISO 27001, AI — my favourite topics, published on LinkedIn, gathered here.
blog.cta_subtitle: 30 minutes to review your compliance status — free, no commitment.

pentest.title: Pentest & Security Audit — AgileVizion
pentest.hero_h1: Is your company exposed? We'll show you in 24 hours. For free.
pentest.hero_subtitle: 25 specialised AI agents scan your attack surface and produce a complete security assessment — score, vulnerabilities, remediation plan. All in plain language.
pentest.problems_title: What you can't see can cost you dearly
pentest.problem_1_text: Forgotten subdomains, open ports, exposed services, default configurations — most SMEs don't know their own attack surface.
pentest.problem_2_text: DORA (Digital Operational Resilience Act) Art. 26 and NIS 2 Art. 14 mandate operational resilience testing. Not testing means non-compliance.
pentest.problem_3_text: Between the publication of a CVE (Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures) and mass exploitation, sometimes less than 48 hours pass. Without monitoring, you're exposed without knowing it.
pentest.diag_subtitle: A security score from 0 to 100, a grade from A+ to E, and the 5 most critical vulnerabilities on your external surface. No installation required.
pentest.diag_1_text: Are your forgotten subdomains accessible? Are internal services exposed to the internet without your knowledge?
pentest.diag_2_text: Are your emails protected against impersonation? SPF, DMARC and DKIM verification — the 3 anti-phishing shields for your domain.
pentest.diag_3_text: Are your SSL/TLS certificates up to date? Is encryption properly configured? Are known vulnerabilities present?
pentest.diag_4_text: Are dangerous ports open to the internet? Do your servers reveal vulnerable software versions?
pentest.diag_5_text: Automatic correlation with CVE databases (NVD, CISA KEV) — we identify known and actively exploited vulnerabilities.
pentest.diag_6_text: Has someone registered a domain name similar to yours to trick your clients or employees?
pentest.offres_subtitle: From quick assessment to continuous monitoring — each tier builds on the previous one.
pentest.offre_1_desc: 100% automated scan of your external surface. Delivered in 24h.
pentest.offre_2_desc: External + internal audit + offensive pentest. All-inclusive.
pentest.offre_2_item_3: On-site WiFi audit + cloud (M365, AWS, Azure)
pentest.offre_2_item_4: OWASP Top 10 on your web applications
pentest.offre_2_item_5: Offensive pentest: CVE exploitation, SQL injection, brute force, AD attack chain
pentest.offre_2_livrable_desc: 25-50 page report + executive summary + remediation plan + MITRE ATT&CK mapping
pentest.offre_3_desc: Automated monthly Flash Assessment + real-time critical alerts.
pentest.offre_3_item_1: Automated monthly scan of your perimeter
pentest.diff_1_text: 25 specialised AI agents working in parallel. Assessment in 24h, fast full audit — versus 2 to 4 weeks at a traditional firm.
pentest.diff_2_text: CVSS v3.1 scoring + MITRE ATT&CK mapping — the same language as the CSSF (Luxembourg's financial regulator) and cyber insurers. Nothing missed, nothing subjective.
pentest.diff_3_text: AI automation cuts costs. A full audit at consulting-firm quality, at a price suited to Luxembourg SMEs.
pentest.methodo_subtitle: A structured, reproducible process aligned with international standards.
pentest.methodo_1_text: OSINT, subdomain enumeration, WHOIS, DNS analysis, typosquatting, Shodan exposure.
pentest.methodo_2_text: Ports, versions, SSL/TLS, HTTP headers, SPF/DMARC/DKIM, CVE correlation, OWASP Top 10.
pentest.methodo_3_text: Offensive pentest: CVE exploitation, SQL injection, brute force, Active Directory attack chain, lateral movement.
pentest.methodo_4_text: Encrypted PDF report, non-technical executive summary, remediation plan prioritised by criticality, MITRE ATT&CK mapping.
pentest.ref_1: MITRE ATT&CK — every finding mapped to the universal framework
pentest.ref_2: CVSS v3.1 — standardised scoring from 0 to 10 for each vulnerability
pentest.ref_3: Global score /100 — visual summary with grade from A+ to E
pentest.ref_4: Engagement agreement signed before any intrusive testing
pentest.conformite_title: Technical auditing completes your regulatory compliance
pentest.conformite_subtitle: Testing your systems is no longer optional — it's a legal obligation.
pentest.conformite_dora: Mandatory digital operational resilience testing for financial entities. TLPT (Threat-Led Penetration Testing) for the most significant ones.
pentest.conformite_nis2: Cyber risk management measures, including regular security testing and auditing of information systems.
pentest.conformite_iso: Technical compliance verification: penetration testing and security review within the ISMS (Information Security Management System) continuous improvement framework.
